entries: {@i Choose A or B}: {@item Leather Armor|XPHB}, 2 {@item Dagger|XPHB|Daggers}, {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} ({@item Rod|XPHB}), {@item Explorer's Pack|XPHB} and 16 GP or (B) 100 GP

classFeature.name: Eidolon
entries: Your soul has become entwined with that of an otherworldly spirit, which acts as your Eidolon. This spirit is no mere minion that you summon, nor is it some loose entity you are tethered to: it is an extension of yourself, and you share an unbreakable bond with it.

entries.name: Manifest Eidolon
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can manifest your Eidolon, causing it to appear in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you with {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Summoner level and one Action Point (see below). The Eidolon has an appearance of your choice and uses the Eidolon stat block. You summon the same Eidolon each time you use this action - if it is already manifested when you use this action, you instead cause it to dissipate. The Eidolon also dissipates if you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or if it is ever more than its maximum range away from you, which is a number of feet equal to 50 times your Summoner level.

entries.name: Equipment
entries: Your Eidolon can attune to magic items, but its attunements count against the number you can attune. When your Eidolon dissipates, any equipment it was carrying remains, with the exception of Magic Items that it is attuned to. If you die, these items appear in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you.

entries.name: Psychic Link
entries: You can communicate telepathically with your Eidolon, even when it is not manifested.

entries.name: Shared Life
entries: You share your vitality with your Eidolon. It does not have {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, and it shares your pool of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. If both of you take damage or are healed simultaneously by the same effect, you are only affected once (by the greater effect). For the purposes of {@status Concentration|XPHB}, your Eidolon taking damage counts as you taking damage, and vice versa.

entries.name: Action Points
entries: Action Points (AP) are a special resource which you can use to empower the Eidolon's actions. On your turn, you can sacrifice your action and/or your {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to grant your Eidolon Action Points. Sacrificing your action grants it two points, and sacrificing your {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} grants it one. It retains these points until the start of your next turn. Ways that it can expend them are listed on its stat block.
The maximum number of Action Points your Eidolon can spend on a single action is equal to 1 plus half your Summoner level (round up).

entries.name: Controlling the Eidolon
entries: In combat, the Eidolon shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} score and acts during your turn. The Eidolon is able to move and take actions on its own, following your mental command, even if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Shared Reaction
entries: Once you or your Eidolon take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, neither of you can take another until the start of your next turn. One of you being unable to take Reactions doesn't prevent the other from being able to.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: The link you share with your Eidolon has granted you the capacity to cast spells. See chapter 7 of the Player's Handbook for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Summoner spells, which appear in the Summoner spell list later in this class's description.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know two cantrips of your choice from the Summoner spell list. {@spell Fire Bolt|XPHB} and {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB} are recommended.
Whenever you gain a Summoner level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the Summoner spell list.
When you reach Summoner levels 4 and 10, you learn another cantrip of your choice from the Summoner spell list, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Summoner Features table.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Summoner Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of Level 1+
entries: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Summoner spells. {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB} and {@spell Longstrider|XPHB} are recommended.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Summoner levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Summoner Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Summoner spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the Summoner Features table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 5 Summoner, your list of prepared spells can include eight Summoner spells of level 1 or 2 in any combination.

If another Summoner feature gives you spells that you may always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Summoner spells for you.

entries.name: Changing Your Prepared Spells
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one spell on your list with another Summoner spell for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Summoner spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Summoner spells.

classFeature.name: Evolution
entries: You have learned to invest magical power into your Eidolon, allowing it to take on new and fantastical powers in the form of Evolutions. You gain two Evolutions of your choice, such as {@optfeature Strike Mastery|SummonerClass24} and {@optfeature Unsummon|SummonerClass24}. Evolutions are described in the "{@filter Evolution Options|optionalfeatures|source=summonerclass24;summonerexpansionpack24|feature type=eo}" section.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: If an Evolution has a prerequisite, you must meet it to gain that Evolution.

entries.name: Replacing and Gaining Evolutions
entries: Whenever you gain a Summoner level, you can replace one of your Evolutions with another one for which you qualify. You can't replace an Evolution if it's a prerequisite for another Evolution that you have. When you gain certain Summoner levels, you gain more Evolutions of your choice, as shown in the Evolutions column of the Summoner Features table.
You can't pick the same Evolution more than once unless its description says otherwise.

classFeature.name: Summoner Subclass
entries: You gain a Summoner subclass of your choice Bond of the Guardian Angel, Bond of the Dragon Herald, Bond of the Elemental Spirit, Bond of the Faerie Trickster, Bond of the Fiendish Chain, Bond of the Symbiote or Bond of the Grave Caller. Subclasses are detailed after this class' description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you special abilities at certain Summoner levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass' features that are of your Summoner level and lower.

classFeature.name: Conjuration Expert
entries: You have two special spell slots, one of level 1 and one of level 2, which are separate from the spell slots of your Spellcasting feature. These spell slots can only be used to cast Conjuration spells. In addition, you can prepare Conjuration spells from the Summoner spell list of a level up to that of these spell slots, even if you would normally not be able to.
The level of these spell slots increases as you gain Summoner levels, shown in the table below.
Finally, whenever you cast a {@filter Summoner spell from the Conjuration school|spells|source=|class=summoner (sc'24)|school=c}, you can ignore any material components that aren't consumed.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Summoner levels 8, 12, and 16. When you gain this feature at levels 8 and 16, you also add +2 to either your Eidolon's Strength, Dexterity or Constitution score, or add +1 to two of those scores, to a maximum of 20.

classFeature.name: Coordinated Maneuvers
entries: You gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Swift Investment
entries: When you use your action to do something other than cast a level 1+ spell, your Eidolon gains 1 Action Point.

entries.name: Coordinated Strike
entries: Once per turn when either you or your Eidolon deals damage to a creature, the other gains {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next attack it makes against that creature before the end of the turn.

classFeature.name: Magical Strikes
entries: Whenever your Eidolon hits with its Conjured Strike attack, it can choose to deal Force damage instead of its normal damage type. This benefit also extends to the attacks of creatures you summon with your spells.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Summoner Subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify. You also add +2 to either your Eidolon's Strength, Dexterity or Constitution score, or add +1 to two of those scores, to a maximum of 20.

classFeature.name: Potent Summons
entries: Whenever you cast a spell that summons one or more creatures, you can empower one of those creatures:

entries.name: Temporary Hit Points
entries: It appears with an amount of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Summoner level.

entries.name: Potent Strikes
entries: It adds {@dice 1d4} to its damage rolls.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Summoner Subclass.

classFeature.name: Strengthened Bond
entries: Your bond with your Eidolon grows ever stronger. You gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Swift Investment
entries: Your Eidolon now gains an Action Point when you use your action to cast a level 1+ spell.

entries.name: Quick Summon
entries: You can now manifest your Eidolon as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Magic Investment
entries: Once per turn, you can expend a spell slot and grant your Eidolon a number of Action Points equal to the spell slot's level (no action required).

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Summoner Subclass.

classFeature.name: Shared Resilience
entries: You and your Eidolon are perfectly in sync - you stay vigilant and protect one another from danger. While you and your Eidolon are within 30 feet of one another, you both have proficiency with all saving throws.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify. You also add +2 to either your Eidolon's Strength, Dexterity or Constitution score, or add +1 to two of those scores, to a maximum of 20.

classFeature.name: Shared Spellcasting
entries: Your magical bond allows your Eidolon to cast your spells. It gains the following action:

entries.name: Spellcasting
entries: The Eidolon casts one of your Summoner spells with an {@variantrule Action|XPHB} casting time, using your pool of spell slots. This action costs a number of AP equal to the spell's level, or 1 AP if it's a cantrip.

If the spell requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}, it concentrates on the spell, but if the combined level of spells that you and your Eidolon are simultaneously {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on ever exceeds 9, one of the spells of your choice immediately ends.

classFeature.name: Epic Boon
entries: You gain an {@filter Epic Boon feat|feats|category=EB} or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Spell Recall|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeature.name: Perfect Synergy
entries: Whenever your Eidolon gains Action Points, the number of points it gains increases by 1.

trait.name: Soul Link
entries: The Eidolon is an extension of your soul. It shares your Intelligence, Wisdom and Charisma scores, and it gains all of your saving throw proficiencies.

trait.name: Action Points
entries: The Eidolon can trigger or enhance certain actions by spending Action Points (AP) if it has any. Taking any action not listed on this stat block costs 1 AP, except for {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, {@action Dodge|XPHB}, {@action Hide|XPHB}, {@action Influence|XPHB} and {@action Search|XPHB}, which cost 0. Taking a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} does not cost AP unless otherwise specified.

action.name: Conjured Strike (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier Bludgeoning, Piercing or Slashing damage (you choose when you Manifest the Eidolon). On a hit, you can increase the damage roll by spending AP, adding {@dice 1d6} for each AP spent.

action.name: Share Senses (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: You project your senses into your Eidolon. Until the start of your next turn, you perceive through it, using its senses while being cut off from your own senses. If you cast a spell while sharing senses in this way, you cast it as though you were in your Eidolon's space. You can stop sharing senses early at any time (no action required). Alternatively, your Eidolon can perceive through your senses, using the same rules.

reaction.name: Opportunity Attack
entries: The Eidolon can make {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} with its Conjured Strike without spending any Action Points.

items.name: Conjured Strike (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier Bludgeoning damage. On a hit, you can increase the damage roll by spending AP, adding {@dice 1d6} for each AP spent.

items.name: Conjured Strike (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier Piercing damage. On a hit, you can increase the damage roll by spending AP, adding {@dice 1d6} for each AP spent.

items.name: Conjured Strike (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier Slashing damage. On a hit, you can increase the damage roll by spending AP, adding {@dice 1d6} for each AP spent.

items.name: Healing Surge
entries: When your Eidolon uses its Conjured Strike, it can forgo one of its attacks and restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to a creature within the attack's range equal to {@dice 2d8} + your Summoner level.

It can use this ability a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once). It regains one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and regains all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Conjured Strike (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier Bludgeoning damage. On a hit, you can increase the damage roll by spending AP, adding {@dice 1d6} for each AP spent.

items.name: Conjured Strike (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier Piercing damage. On a hit, you can increase the damage roll by spending AP, adding {@dice 1d6} for each AP spent.

items.name: Conjured Strike (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier Slashing damage. On a hit, you can increase the damage roll by spending AP, adding {@dice 1d6} for each AP spent.

items.name: Breath Weapon (Cost: 1+ AP)
entries: Dexterity {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}: DC equals your spell save DC, each creature in a 15 foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} or a 30 foot long, 5 foot wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}. {@i Failure:} {@damage 2d6} damage of its Draconic Type. The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} per AP beyond the first. {@i Success:} Half damage.
The base damage increases by {@dice 1d6} at level 6 ({@dice 3d6}) and level 14 ({@dice 4d6}) in this class.

The Eidolon can use this action a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once). It regains one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Elemental Bond
entries: While the Eidolon is manifested, whenever you cast a Summoner spell that deals damage you can change the damage type to that of your Eidolon's Draconic Type.

items.name: Invisibility (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: The Eidolon gains the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until it makes an attack, forces a creature to make a saving throw, or has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. It can also choose to end the effect early (no action required).

items.name: Searing Pain (Cost: 0 AP)
entries: The Eidolon festers the wounds of one creature that you or it has dealt damage to on this turn. The creature takes {@damage 1d4} Fire damage plus half your Summoner level (round up).

items.name: Amorphous
entries: The Eidolon can squeeze through a gap as small as one square inch.

items.name: Elemental Type
entries: It gains the following benefits depending on its elemental type, shown in the Elemental Types table. The Eidolon gains {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the damage type in the Damage column, and its Conjured Strike can deal damage of that type, instead of its normal damage type.

entries.caption: Elemental Types
rows: The Eidolon has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, though it cannot fly while Grappling a creature or while mounted.
The Eidolon gains a +2 bonus to its AC.
A creature hit by the Eidolon's Conjured Strike begins {@hazard Burning|XPHB}. The Eidolon instantly dissipates if it is submerged in water.
The Eidolon has a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can breathe underwater.

items.name: Elemental Type (Air)
entries: The Eidolon has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, though it cannot fly while Grappling a creature or while mounted.

items.name: Conjured Strike (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier Bludgeoning, Piercing, Slashing (you choose when you Manifest the Eidolon), or Thunder damage. On a hit, you can increase the damage roll by spending AP, adding {@dice 1d6} for each AP spent.

items.name: Elemental Type (Earth)
entries: The Eidolon gains a +2 bonus to its AC.

items.name: Elemental Type (Fire)
entries: A creature hit by the Eidolon's Conjured Strike begins {@hazard Burning|XPHB}. The Eidolon instantly dissipates if it is submerged in water.

items.name: Conjured Strike (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier Bludgeoning, Piercing, Slashing (you choose when you Manifest the Eidolon), or Fire damage. On a hit, you can increase the damage roll by spending AP, adding {@dice 1d6} for each AP spent.

items.name: Elemental Type (Water)
entries: The Eidolon has a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can breathe underwater.

items.name: Conjured Strike (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier Bludgeoning, Piercing, Slashing (you choose when you Manifest the Eidolon), or Cold damage. On a hit, you can increase the damage roll by spending AP, adding {@dice 1d6} for each AP spent.

items.name: Enwrap (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: Strength {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}: DC equals your spell save DC, on a creature the Eidolon has just hit with a melee attack. {@i Failure:} The target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Eidolon (escape DC equals your spell save DC). The Eidolon can only have one creature Grappled in this way at a time.

items.name: Undead Vessel
entries: When you choose this subclass, choose whether it is a Corpse or a Spirit. It gains the following {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} depending on your choice:

entries.name: Undead Fortitude (Corpse)
entries: When the Eidolon drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} as a result of it taking damage of a type other than Radiant, it makes a Constitution save, with DC 10 or half the damage taken, whichever is higher. On a success, it drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

entries.name: Partially Incorporeal (Spirit)
entries: When the Eidolon takes damage of a type other than Radiant, it reduces that instance of damage by half.

The Eidolon can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once). You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: When the Eidolon drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} as a result of it taking damage of a type other than Radiant, it makes a Constitution save, with DC 10 or half the damage taken, whichever is higher. On a success, it drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

The Eidolon can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once). You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Partially Incorporeal
entries: When the Eidolon takes damage of a type other than Radiant, it reduces that instance of damage by half.

The Eidolon can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once). You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

hp.special: 60 or 70 (Hill only) + 15 for each spell level above 6

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The spirit makes a number of Giant Weapon attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down). It can replace one of these attacks with its Archetypal Strike.

action.name: Giant Weapon
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d12 + 5 + summonSpellLevel} Bludgeoning damage (Cloud, Fire, Hill and Stone only), Cold damage (Frost only), or Lightning damage (Storm only).

action.name: Archetypal Strike
entries: {@actSave dex} DC equals your spell save DC, one creature or object within 30 ft. {@actSaveFail} {@damage 1d10 + 5 + summonSpellLevel} in damage of the same type as the spirit's Giant Weapon attack.
{@actSaveSuccessOrFail} The way that this effect manifests depends on the spirit's type, providing additional effects:

{@b Cloud:} The spirit strikes with conjured, solidified cloud. Immediately before or after making the attack, the spirit {@variantrule Teleportation|XPHB|Teleports} up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.
{@b Fire:} The spirit strikes with siege weaponry, dealing double damage against objects and structures.
{@b Frost:} The spirit strikes with a conjured hunk of ice. In addition, it coats its body in a frosty layer of ice. It gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the spell's level.

{@b Hill:} The spirit leaps to the target's space and lands {@condition Prone|XPHB} on top of it. If the target succeeds on its saving throw, the giant instead lands on the nearest unoccupied space. While the spirit is {@condition Prone|XPHB} in its space, the target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} and {@condition Prone|XPHB} conditions (escape DC = your spell save DC). A target automatically makes its save if it is Huge or larger.
{@b Stone:} The spirit strikes by hurling a rock at the target. On a failed save, it is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

{@b Storm:} The spirit strikes the target with a bolt of lightning. Each other creature within 5 feet of the target must also make the saving throw, taking Lightning damage equal to the spell's level on a failed save.

optionalfeature.name: Eidolon Mount
entries: Your Eidolon becomes suitable for you to mount, provided it is the same size as you or larger. It acts as an Independent Mount, but remains under your control, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is not reduced as a result of you riding it.

optionalfeature.name: Energy Attunement
entries: Select one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison or Thunder. Your Eidolon gains {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the chosen damage type, and when it hits with its Conjured Strike, it can choose to deal damage of that type instead of its normal damage type.

optionalfeature.name: Enhanced Movement
entries: Your Eidolon's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. In addition, it gains the following {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}:

entries.name: Movement Surge (Cost: 1AP)
entries: The Eidolon takes the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB} or {@action Dodge|XPHB} action.

optionalfeature.name: Ranged Attacker
entries: When your Eidolon uses its Conjured Strike, it can choose to make it as a ranged attack roll, with a normal range of 30 feet and long range of 90 feet. The d6s of Conjured Strike are reduced to d4s when you do so.

optionalfeature.name: Size Alteration
entries: Your Eidolon's size increases or decreases by one category. If you make it larger, its Strength score increases by 1, and if you make it smaller, its Dexterity score increases by 1.

You can choose this Evolution multiple times, but cannot use it to make your Eidolon smaller than Tiny or larger than Huge, and you can't both increase and decrease its size to gain both ability score increases.

optionalfeature.name: Strike Mastery
entries: Select one of the following Weapon Mastery properties: {@itemMastery Push|XPHB}, {@itemMastery Sap|XPHB}, {@itemMastery Slow|XPHB}, {@itemMastery Topple|XPHB} or {@itemMastery Vex|XPHB}. Your Eidolon's Conjured Strike attack gains the chosen Mastery Property. You can exchange your choice for another whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Tethered Space
entries: Your Eidolon gains the following action:

entries.name: Swap Places (Cost: 1AP)
entries: You and your Eidolon both {@variantrule Teleportation|XPHB|Teleport}, swapping places with one another.

optionalfeature.name: Unsummon
entries: Your Eidolon gains the following {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}:

entries.name: Unsummon
entries: Trigger: The Eidolon takes damage. Response: The Eidolon dissipates immediately before taking the damage.

optionalfeature.name: Aquatic Body
entries: Your Eidolon gains a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can breathe underwater.

optionalfeature.name: Double Attack
entries: When your Eidolon uses its Conjured Strike, it can now make up to two attacks. It must spend Action Points to make and empower each attack separately.

optionalfeature.name: Enhanced Resilience
entries: Your Eidolon gains a +2 bonus to its AC and you add your Charisma modifier (minimum 1) to the {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} it gains when you manifest it.

optionalfeature.name: Grappling Strike
entries: Once per turn when your Eidolon hits with its Conjured Strike, it can immediately make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against the target of the attack, taking the Grapple option. The DC is your spell save DC.

optionalfeature.name: Spell Transference
entries: Whenever you cast a spell that targets only yourself, you can instead target your Eidolon with it, as if it had cast the spell on itself. If the spell requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}, you must still concentrate on it.

optionalfeature.name: Untether
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can remove your Eidolon's maximum range for up to 1 hour, or until you lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} (as if {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on a spell).

optionalfeature.name: Arcane Sight
entries: Your Eidolon gains {@sense Truesight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Flight
entries: Your Eidolon gains a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Your Eidolon has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects. If you make such a saving throw while the Eidolon is manifested, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to also gain this benefit for that roll.

optionalfeature.name: Melded Senses
entries: The effects of your Eidolon's 'Share Senses' action now last indefinitely, and you are not cut off from your own senses while it is active. Furthermore, while you and your Eidolon are within line of sight of one another, you can both ignore the effects of the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition, provided at least one of you lacks that condition.

optionalfeature.name: Triple Attack
entries: When your Eidolon uses its Conjured Strike, it can now make up to three attacks. It must spend Action Points to make and empower each attack separately.

optionalfeature.name: Flexible Evolution
entries: Select one Evolution that your Eidolon does not have and that it meets the prerequisites for. The Evolution cannot have a prerequisite of a level higher than 7.
Your Eidolon gains that Evolution and whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can exchange it for another eligible option.

optionalfeature.name: Reposition
entries: When your Eidolon uses its {@optfeature Unsummon|SummonerClass24} {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, it can choose to {@variantrule Teleportation|XPHB|Teleport} to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you instead of dissipating. It can only do so if it is not already within 5 feet of you. If the triggering damage was caused by an area of effect, and its destination is also in the area, this {@variantrule Teleportation|XPHB|Teleport} does not allow the Eidolon to avoid taking the damage.

optionalfeature.name: Skill Expert
entries: Choose two skills with which the Eidolon has proficiency. It has {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} with those two skills.

optionalfeature.name: Terrifying Presence
entries: Your Eidolon gains the following action:

entries.name: Terrify (Cost: 2 AP)
entries: Wisdom {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}: DC equals your spell save DC. One creature within 15 ft. Failure: The target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. It can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the condition on a success.
The Eidolon can use this action a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and regains all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Enchanting Presence
entries: Your Eidolon gains the following action:

entries.name: Enamor (Cost: 2 AP)
entries: Wisdom {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}: DC equals your spell save DC. One creature within 15 ft. Failure: The target is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by you and the Eidolon for 1 minute. It can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the condition on a success.
The Eidolon can use this action a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and regains all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}

optionalfeature.name: Skillful
entries: The Eidolon gains proficiency with two skills of your choice. You can select this Evolution more than once.

optionalfeature.name: Reaching Strike
entries: The reach of your Eidolon's Conjured Strike increases to 10 feet, if it wasn't already longer. In addition, the Eidolon has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

optionalfeature.name: Floating Body
entries: Your Eidolon has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can {@variantrule hover|XPHB} above solid and liquid surfaces. If the Eidolon is ever more than 10 feet off the ground using this {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, it falls until it is 10 feet off the ground.
If the Eidolon has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} from another source, that {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} gains the {@variantrule hover|XPHB} trait.

item.name: Amulet of the Spirit Caller
entries: This amulet has 5 charges, and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. When you cast a summoning spell while wearing this amulet, you can expend charges to improve the summoned creature in one of the following ways per expended charge. You cannot select the same benefit more than once.

items: It appears with 20 extra {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
It adds {@dice 1d4} to each of its damage rolls.
It gains a +2 bonus to its AC.

entries: A spell is only eligible to receive this enhancement if it requires {@status Concentration|XPHB} and only summons a single creature.

item.name: Bands of Synergy
entries: These rings come as a pair: one worn by a Summoner, and the other worn by their Eidolon. While wearing these rings, the bond between you and your Eidolon is strengthened. Once per turn when your Eidolon uses an action that requires Action Points while it is within 30 feet of you, it can reduce the AP cost by 1.

item.name: Bondmantle
entries: While wearing this mantle, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to gain one of your Eidolon's traits, selecting from the following list:

items: One of its {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immunities}

entries: You retain the trait until you use the action again.

item.name: Sphere of the Arcane Armament
entries: This mysterious hunk of smooth metal is surprisingly malleable and ductile, but when left alone, slowly returns to a perfectly spherical shape.

In order to attune to this item, the Eidolon or its Summoner must also be holding a magic weapon. Once attuned, the weapon disappears, and the sphere reshapes into a weapon appropriate for the Eidolon to attack with. For example, if the Eidolon attacks with its claws, the sphere forms a sort of clawed gauntlet, or if it attacks with a weapon, it forms into a replica of that weapon.

The Eidolon's Conjured Strike gains the magical properties of the magic weapon used as part of the attunement. If the weapon required attunement, the Eidolon is considered attuned to it, though it doesn't count against the number of items it can have attuned, and it must meet any prerequisites to attune.
For example, if the weapon was a Flame Tongue, the Eidolon would gain the ability to set its weapon ablaze and deal extra damage with its Conjured Strike.
When the attunement ends, the weapon and the sphere reappear within 5 feet of the Eidolon.

item.name: +1 Soulbind Chains
entries: These fine chains can be worn as a necklace or a bracelet, and they dimly glow while your Eidolon is near. You can use the chains as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your spells while wearing it, and you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls ands to the saving throw DCs of your Summoner spells.

In addition, when you sacrifice your action or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to grant your Eidolon Action Points, you can increase the number of Action Points it gains by 2, provided it is within 30 feet of you. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: +2 Soulbind Chains
entries: These fine chains can be worn as a necklace or a bracelet, and they dimly glow while your Eidolon is near. You can use the chains as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your spells while wearing it, and you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls ands to the saving throw DCs of your Summoner spells.

In addition, when you sacrifice your action or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to grant your Eidolon Action Points, you can increase the number of Action Points it gains by 2, provided it is within 30 feet of you. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: +3 Soulbind Chains
entries: These fine chains can be worn as a necklace or a bracelet, and they dimly glow while your Eidolon is near. You can use the chains as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your spells while wearing it, and you gain a +3 bonus to spell attack rolls ands to the saving throw DCs of your Summoner spells.

In addition, when you sacrifice your action or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to grant your Eidolon Action Points, you can increase the number of Action Points it gains by 2, provided it is within 30 feet of you. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Evolution Stone (Rare)
entries: If the stone is held by a Summoner's Eidolon for 24 hours, it permanently gains that Evolution, even if it doesn't meet the prerequisites. If it already knows the Evolution, it can instantly swap it for for another one it meets the prerequisites for. The stone then turns into a mundane crystal, worth {@dice 1d4 x 100} GP.

item.name: Evolution Stone (Very Rare)
entries: If the stone is held by a Summoner's Eidolon for 24 hours, it permanently gains that Evolution, even if it doesn't meet the prerequisites. If it already knows the Evolution, it can instantly swap it for for another one it meets the prerequisites for. The stone then turns into a mundane crystal, worth {@dice 1d4 x 100} GP.

item.name: Evolution Stone (Legendary)
entries: If the stone is held by a Summoner's Eidolon for 24 hours, it permanently gains that Evolution, even if it doesn't meet the prerequisites. If it already knows the Evolution, it can instantly swap it for for another one it meets the prerequisites for. The stone then turns into a mundane crystal, worth {@dice 1d4 x 100} GP.

itemGroup.name: Evolution Stone
entries: This fist-sized hunk of crystal faintly glows, hinting at an unrealised potential. The stone stores one {@filter Evolution option|optionalfeatures|source=summonerclass24;summonerexpansionpack24|feature type=eo} from the Summoner class. The DM chooses which Evolution it contains, which determines its rarity (see the table below).

If the stone is held by a Summoner's Eidolon for 24 hours, it permanently gains that Evolution, even if it doesn't meet the prerequisites. If it already knows the Evolution, it can instantly swap it for for another one it meets the prerequisites for. The stone then turns into a mundane crystal, worth {@dice 1d4 x 100} GP.

rows: Evolutions with a level 7+ prerequisite and no other prerequisites
Monstrous Evolutions; Evolutions with a level 13+ prerequisite and no other prerequisites

spell.name: Summon Giant

m.text: a stone slate inscribed with giant runes, worth 600+ GP

entries: You call forth the glorious spirit of a giant. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range and uses the {@creature Giant Spirit|SummonerSpells24} stat block. When you cast the spell, choose a type: Cloud, Fire, Frost, Hill, Stone or Storm. The creature resembles a giant of its type, which determines certain details in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

spell.name: Summon Monstrosity

m.text: A gilded claw of a Monstrosity worth 300+ GP

entries: You call forth the essence of a classical monster. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range and uses the {@creature Monstrosity Spirit|SummonerSpells24} stat block. When you cast the spell, choose a form: Displacer Beast, Manticore or Owlbear. The creature resembles a Monstrosity of that kind, which determines certain details in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

spell.name: Summon Ooze

m.text: a container filled with the remains of an ooze worth 400+ GP

entries: You call forth the essence of a mindless ooze. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range and uses the {@creature Ooze Spirit|SummonerSpells24} stat block. When you cast the spell, choose a form: Gelatinous {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}, Jelly or Oblex. The creature resembles an amorphous ooze, with a coloration of your choosing, and characteristics marked by the chosen form. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

spell.name: Summon Plant

m.text: a rare plant preserved in amber, worth 300+ GP

entries: You call forth the spirit of a monstrous prehensile plant. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range and uses the {@creature Plant Spirit|SummonerSpells24} stat block. When you cast the spell, choose Blight, Myconid or Treant. The creature resembles a plant creature of its type, which determines certain details in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

spell.name: Summon Titan

time.note: casting as an action gives you 1 level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}

m.text: Platinum-coated remnant of a Titan, worth 999+ GP

entries: You call forth the magical manifestation of a Titan. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range and uses the {@creature Titan Spirit|SummonerSpells24} stat block. When you cast the spell, choose a type: Astral Dreadnought, Kraken or Tarrasque. The creature resembles a creature of its type, which determines certain details in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

subclass.name: Bond of the Dragon Herald
subclassFeatures: Bond of the Dragon Herald|Summoner|SummonerClass24|Dragon Herald|SummonerClass24|3

subclassFeatures: Bond of the Faerie Trickster|Summoner|SummonerClass24|Faerie Trickster|SummonerClass24|3

subclassFeatures: Bond of the Elemental Spirit|Summoner|SummonerClass24|Elemental Spirit|SummonerClass24|3

subclassFeatures: Bond of the Fiendish Chain|Summoner|SummonerClass24|Fiendish Chain|SummonerClass24|3
Protection of the Lower Planes|Summoner|SummonerClass24|Fiendish Chain|SummonerClass24|10

subclass.name: Bond of the Grave Caller
subclassFeatures: Bond of the Grave Caller|Summoner|SummonerClass24|Grave Caller|SummonerClass24|3

subclassFeature.name: Bond of the Guardian Angel
entries: {@i Defend your Allies with a Celestial Spirit}

You have formed an everlasting bond with a good-aligned creature of the upper planes. Thanks to this connection, you and your Eidolon overflow with healing light. Your Celestial Eidolon likely upholds a strict moral code, one which you must navigate in order to effectively work in tandem.

subclassFeature.name: Celestial Spells
entries: When you reach a Summoner level specified in the Celestial Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Celestial Spells
rows: {@spell Mass Healing Word|XPHB}, {@spell Revivify|XPHB}, {@spell Summon Celestial|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Celestial Eidolon
entries: Your Eidolon manifests as a celestial originating from the upper planes, taking the general appearance of an angel or some other celestial creature. Your Eidolon's stat block changes in the following ways:

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Its creature type changes to Celestial.

entries.name: Ability Scores
entries: Its Strength, Dexterity and Constitution scores change to 14 (+2), 13 (+1) and 16 (+3) respectively.

entries.name: Skills
entries: It gains proficiency with the {@skill Insight|XPHB} and {@skill Medicine|XPHB} skills.

entries.name: Resistances
entries: It has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic and Radiant damage.

entries.name: Healing Surge
entries: When your Eidolon uses its Conjured Strike, it can forgo one of its attacks and restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to a creature within the attack's range equal to {@dice 2d8} + your Summoner level.

It can use this ability a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once). It regains one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and regains all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Exalted Presence
entries: Your connection to your Celestial Eidolon grants you an augmented presence. You gain proficiency with your choice of the {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} skills. If you already have your chosen proficiency, you instead gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB}.
In addition, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks made to discern whether a creature is lying.

subclassFeature.name: Mortal Sacrifice
entries: Whenever you see a creature within 30 feet of you get hit by an attack while your Eidolon is manifested, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to {@variantrule Teleportation|XPHB|Teleport} your Eidolon to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target, and it instead gets hit by the attack. The Eidolon has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the attack's damage.
You can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once) and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Prodigious Healer
entries: When your Eidolon restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to a creature with its {@variantrule Healing|XPHB} Surge, it can also remove one of the following conditions from it: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}. If it doesn't remove any conditions, it instead adds your Charisma modifier (minimum +1) to the healing roll.

subclassFeature.name: Immortal Bulwark
entries: At the start of each of your turns, your Eidolon gains a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your Summoner level.
In addition, you are no longer limited in the number of times you can use your Mortal Sacrifice feature.

subclassFeature.name: Bond of the Dragon Herald
entries: {@i Wreak Draconic Devastation}

You have become bound to an intensely magical draconic spirit. These spirits often manifest following the death of a true dragon, and latch onto Summoners seeking some way of manifesting a physical body. These creatures run the gamut in terms of morality, some seeking the subjugation of their Summoner companion, while others have immense deference to the companion who grants them a continued life.

subclassFeature.name: Draconic Spells
entries: When you reach a Summoner level specified in the Draconic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Draconic Spells
rows: {@spell Fireball|XPHB}, {@spell Protection from Energy|XPHB}, {@spell Summon Dragon|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Dragon Eidolon
entries: Your Eidolon manifests as a Medium sized dragon, standing on four legs but without wings. Your Eidolon's stat block changes in the following ways:

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Its creature type changes to Dragon.

entries.name: Ability Scores
entries: Its Strength, Dexterity and Constitution scores change to 16 (+3), 13 (+1) and 14 (+2) respectively.

entries.name: Skills
entries: It gains proficiency with the {@skill Perception|XPHB} and {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} skills.

entries.name: Draconic Type
entries: When you choose this subclass, select what kind of dragon it is from the Draconic Type table. The Eidolon has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the listed damage type.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: It gains the following action:

entries.name: Breath Weapon (Cost: 1+ AP)
entries: Dexterity {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}: DC equals your spell save DC, each creature in a 15 foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} or a 30 foot long, 5 foot wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}. {@i Failure:} {@damage 2d6} damage of its Draconic Type. The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} per AP beyond the first. {@i Success:} Half damage.
The base damage increases by {@dice 1d6} at level 6 ({@dice 3d6}) and level 14 ({@dice 4d6}) in this class.

The Eidolon can use this action a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once). It regains one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Elemental Bond
entries: While the Eidolon is manifested, whenever you cast a Summoner spell that deals damage you can change the damage type to that of your Eidolon's Draconic Type.

subclassFeature.name: Evocation Expert
entries: The benefits of your Conjuration Expert feature also extend to Evocation spells.
In addition, Evocation spells on the Wizard spell list are added to the Summoner spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Summoner's Blast
entries: Your connection with your Draconic Eidolon has awakened the destructive potential of your magic.

entries.name: Empowered Blast
entries: When you cast a level 1+ Summoner spell that deals damage, you can add your Charisma modifier (minimum +1) to one of the spell's damage rolls.

entries.name: Free Casting
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, select one Summoner spell of the Evocation school (Evocation spells in the Draconic Spells table are eligible choices). The spell can be of a level up to half your Summoner level (round up, maximum level 5). Once before finishing your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can cast your selected spell without expending a spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Breath Weapon
entries: When you cast a level 1+ spell that deals damage of your Eidolon's Draconic Type, your Eidolon's Breath Weapon is empowered until the end of your turn. Its area increases to a 30 foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} or a 60 foot {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}, and it adds your Charisma modifier (minimum +1) to the damage roll.

subclassFeature.name: Draconic Realisation
entries: Your Eidolon inherits some of the traits of true dragons, gaining the following benefits:

entries.name: Fear Immunity
entries: It is {@variantrule Immune|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Wings
entries: It has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet.

entries.name: Elemental Epitome
entries: It is {@variantrule Immune|XPHB} to the damage type of its Draconic Type, and its Conjured Strike deals an extra {@dice 1d6} damage of that type on a hit.

subclassFeature.name: Bond of the Faerie Trickster
entries: {@i Beguile Foes with Tricks and Pranks}

You have entered into an eternal partnership with a tricksy fey creature. Many Summoners bond with these creatures so do by accident, being bound via trickily worded questions. These Eidolons revel in the delights of pranks and charms—consider whether you indulge in this behavior or try to rein in its chaotic tendencies.

subclassFeature.name: Fey Spells
entries: When you reach a Summoner level specified in the Fey Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Fey Spells
rows: {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB}, {@spell Phantasmal Force|XPHB}, {@spell Summon Fey|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Faerie Eidolon
entries: Your Eidolon manifests as a pixie, sprite or some other Tiny winged humanoid, overflowing with fey magic. Your Eidolon's stat block changes in the following ways:

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Its creature type changes to Fey.

entries.name: Size
entries: It stands approximately 3–8 inches tall. Its Size changes to Tiny.

entries.name: Ability Scores
entries: Its Strength, Dexterity and Constitution scores change to 8 (-1), 16 (+3) and 14 (+2) respectively.

entries.name: Skills
entries: It gains proficiency with the {@skill Deception|XPHB} and {@skill Stealth|XPHB} skills.

entries.name: Speed
entries: It gains a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entries.name: Invisibility (Cost: 1 AP)
entries: The Eidolon gains the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until it makes an attack, forces a creature to make a saving throw, or has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. It can also choose to end the effect early (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Enchantment Expert
entries: The benefits of your Conjuration Expert feature also extend to Enchantment spells.
In addition, Enchantment spells on the Wizard spell list are added to the Summoner spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Faerie Dust
entries: Your Eidolon gains the following action:

{@b Faerie Dust (Cost: 1 AP).} The Eidolon sprinkles some of its enchanted Faerie Dust to bless or prank a creature within 5 feet of it. If the target is friendly to you, it gains a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can hover. Otherwise, roll on the Faerie Dust table to determine the effect. In either case, the effect lasts for 1 minute, and ends early if the Eidolon dissipates or uses this action again.

entries.caption: Faerie Dust
rows: The target must succeed on a Wisdom save or be affected by the {@spell Sleep|XPHB} spell.
The target must succeed on a Wisdom save or be affected by the {@spell Tasha's Hideous Laughter|XPHB} spell.

The target must succeed on a Constitution save or be affected by the Reduce effect of the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} spell. It can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
The target must succeed on a Wisdom save or be affected by the {@spell Levitate|XPHB} spell. It can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
The target must succeed on a Wisdom save or be affected by the {@spell Charm Monster|XPHB} spell, as if you had cast it.
The target must succeed on a Wisdom save or be affected by the {@spell Confusion|XPHB} spell.

entries: These effects use your spell save DC. The Eidolon can take this action a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once) and regains all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Fey Suggestion
entries: While a creature is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by you, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to suggest a course of action to it, provided you or your Eidolon is within 5 feet of it. On its next turn, the target precisely follows your command. It is able to take its action and Bonus Action even if it has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition as a result of the effect charming it.
At the end of its turn, the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition ends on the target, and it has Immunity to this effect for 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Ultimate Trickster
entries: Your Eidolon improves in the following ways:

entries.name: Spreading Dust
entries: The range of your Eidolon's Faerie Dust action increases to 30 feet. Furthermore, when it uses its Faerie Dust, it can expend additional Action Points to target more creatures with it at a rate of one creature per point. Each creature is affected by the same effect on the Faerie Dust table.

entries.name: Improved Invisibility
entries: The Eidolon's Invisibility trait no longer ends as a result of making an attack or forcing a saving throw, provided it misses the attack, or the target succeeds on its save.

subclassFeature.name: Bond of the Elemental Spirit
entries: {@i Call on Spirits of the Four Elements}

You have attuned yourself to the four Elemental Planes, and can now call on its denizens to serve you. Unlike other Summoners, you do not necessarily conjure the same Eidolon each time, calling on a variety of creatures representing the four elements. Your practice has forced you to become adept at managing these wild and chaotic creatures, but your reward is command over a wide variety of elemental powers.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Spells
entries: When you reach a Summoner level specified in the Elemental Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Eidolon
entries: Whenever you manifest your Eidolon it appears as an Air, Earth, Fire or Water elemental (you choose which each time you manifest it). Your Eidolon's stat block changes in the following ways:

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Its creature type changes to Elemental.

entries.name: Ability Scores
entries: Its Strength, Dexterity and Constitution scores change to 14 (+2), 13 (+1) and 16 (+3) respectively.

entries.name: Amorphous
entries: The Eidolon can squeeze through a gap as small as one square inch.

entries.name: Elemental Type
entries: It gains the following benefits depending on its elemental type, shown in the Elemental Types table. The Eidolon gains {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the damage type in the Damage column, and its Conjured Strike can deal damage of that type, instead of its normal damage type.

entries.caption: Elemental Types
rows: The Eidolon has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, though it cannot fly while Grappling a creature or while mounted.
The Eidolon gains a +2 bonus to its AC.
A creature hit by the Eidolon's Conjured Strike begins {@hazard Burning|XPHB}. The Eidolon instantly dissipates if it is submerged in water.
The Eidolon has a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can breathe underwater.

subclassFeature.name: Primordial Attunement
entries: Thanks to your connection to your Elemental Eidolon, you know the {@language Primordial|XPHB} language and its dialects ({@language Aquan|XPHB}, {@language Auran|XPHB}, {@language Ignan|XPHB} and {@language Terran|XPHB}).

In addition, if you cast {@spell Elementalism|XPHB} while your Eidolon is manifested, the size of the area you can beckon or sculpt is tripled, and in all dimensions it is tied to your Eidolon's current Elemental Type.

subclassFeature.name: Swap Elements
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can change your Eidolon's Elemental Type to one of the other kinds. When you do, it gains 1 Action Point and the following effect occurs, depending on its new type:

entries.name: Air
entries: The Eidolon emits a burst of rushing air. Each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a Strength save or be pushed 10 feet away from it.

entries.name: Earth
entries: The Eidolon causes the ground within 10 feet of its current space to crack and sunder, becoming {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.

entries.name: Fire
entries: The Eidolon emits a burst of flame. Each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a Dexterity save or take {@damage 2d6} Fire damage, or half as much on a success. This damage increases to {@dice 3d6} at level 10 and to {@dice 4d6} at level 14.

entries.name: Water
entries: The Eidolon emerges in a wave of water. Each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a Strength save or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

These effects use your spell save DC. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Remodement
entries: Your Eidolon's Elemental Type damage Resisance becomes an {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}, and its traits improve in the following ways:

entries.name: Air
entries: Its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} increases to 60 feet, and it is no longer restricted from {@variantrule Grappling|XPHB} or being mounted.

entries.name: Earth
entries: It has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Piercing and Slashing damage.

entries.name: Fire
entries: When it causes a target to start {@hazard Burning|XPHB}, the damage of the {@hazard Burning|XPHB} hazard increases to {@dice 1d10}.

entries.name: Water
entries: When it hits a creature with its Conjured Strike, it can use its {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against the target, taking the Grapple or Shove option. The {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} uses your spell save DC.

subclassFeature.name: Twin Eidolons
entries: When you manifest your Eidolon, you can temporarily conjure two Eidolons at the same time. If your Eidolon is already present, you instead manifest a second one.

entries.name: Different Elements
entries: The two Eidolons cannot have the same Elemental Type.

entries.name: Action Points
entries: Sacrificing your action or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} grants Action Points to both Eidolons.

entries.name: Swap Elements
entries: If you use your Swap Elements feature, you choose whether one or both of the Eidolons change their Elemental Type.

entries.name: Magic Items
entries: Only one of the Eidolons appears with its attuned magic items.

entries.name: Duration
entries: Your magic can only sustain the two Eidolons for up to 10 minutes, after which time one Eidolon of your choice dissipates. You then can't use this feature again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Alternatively, you can restore its use by expending a level 4+ spell slot (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Bond of the Fiendish Chain
entries: {@i Inflict Pain with a Malevolent Fiend}

You have bound yourself to a fiend from the Lower Planes, a being of malice and torment. This relationship may have been forged as a pact with a lesser devil, or as a twisted punishment by a demon lord. The fiend that acts as your Eidolon yearns to corrupt and destroy—consider whether this desire influences you into indulging in carnage, or strengthens your resolve against it.

subclassFeature.name: Fiend Spells
entries: When you reach a Summoner level specified in the Fiendish Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Fiend Eidolon
entries: Your Eidolon manifests as a fiend, taking the general appearance of a devil, demon or yugoloth. Your Eidolon's stat block changes in the following ways:

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Its creature type changes to Fiend.

entries.name: Ability Scores
entries: Its Strength, Dexterity and Constitution scores change to 16 (+3), 13 (+1) and 14 (+2) respectively.

entries.name: Skills
entries: It gains proficiency with the {@skill Deception|XPHB} and {@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB} skills.

entries.name: Resistances
entries: It has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire and Poison damage.

entries.name: Searing Pain
entries: It gains the following {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}:

{@b Searing Pain (Cost: 0 AP).} The Eidolon festers the wounds of one creature that you or it has dealt damage to on this turn. The creature takes {@damage 1d4} Fire damage plus half your Summoner level (round up).

subclassFeature.name: Honorary Fiend
entries: Your connection to your Fiendish Eidolon enhances your sway over other creatures. You learn either the {@language Infernal|XPHB} or {@language Abyssal|XPHB} language (your choice).

In addition, when you fail a Charisma check using a skill, you can reroll the check and must use the new roll, potentially turning it into a success. Once this feature turns a failed check into a success, you cannot use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Hellish Ferocity
entries: Once per turn, when you deal damage to a creature with a spell, your Eidolon gains 1 Action Point.

subclassFeature.name: Protection of the Lower Planes
entries: Your Eidolon becomes resilient to certain effects, and some of that power extends to you.

entries.name: Damage Resilience
entries: Your Eidolon's {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire and Poison damage becomes {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to those damage types.

entries.name: Condition Resilience
entries: Your Eidolon gains {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions.

entries.name: Wrath of the Lower Planes
entries: When your Eidolon uses Searing Pain, it brands the target until the start of your next turn. If a branded creature deals damage to you or your Eidolon, your Eidolon can use Searing Pain on it as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. When it does, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on any subsequent attacks against either of you until the brand ends.
It can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and regains all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Dark Versatility
entries: You have learned to channel the morphological diversity of fiends into your Eidolon. Your Eidolon gains two Evolutions of your choice for which you qualify. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can exchange your chosen Evolutions for another. These selections don't count against the number of Evolutions you know, nor do they qualify you for any Evolutions that list your choices as a prerequisite.

subclassFeature.name: Bond of the Symbiote
entries: {@i Fuse with the Ooze to Become Something Greater}
You have bound your soul to a strange oozy entity which relies on your life essence to survive. In exchange, it offers you incredible physical capabilities. All you have to do is let it in.

subclassFeature.name: Symbiote Spells
entries: When you reach a Summoner level specified in the Symbiote Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Symbiote Eidolon
entries: Your Eidolon manifests as an amorphous blob, seemingly composed of a living liquid material. Your Eidolon's stat block changes in the following ways:

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Its creature type changes to Ooze.

entries.name: Ability Scores
entries: Its Strength, Dexterity and Constitution scores change to 13 (+1), 14 (+2) and 16 (+3) respectively.

entries.name: Skills
entries: It gains proficiency with the {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} and {@skill Survival|XPHB} skills.

entries.name: Enwrap
entries: It gains the following {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}:

{@b Enwrap (Cost: 1 AP).} Strength {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}: DC equals your spell save DC, on a creature the Eidolon has just hit with a melee attack. {@i Failure:} The target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Eidolon (escape DC equals your spell save DC). The Eidolon can only have one creature Grappled in this way at a time.

subclassFeature.name: Merge Bodies
entries: While your Eidolon is within 5 feet of you or is not manifested, you can use your Manifest Eidolon action to merge your body with it. If the Eidolon was manifested, it disappears into your body. Merging with your Eidolon imparts the following effects on you:

entries.name: Statistics
entries: You gain all of your Eidolon's skill proficiencies, its Resistances and Immunities to damage types, you can use any of its actions, and you gain the benefits of its Evolutions. The Eidolon is not considered manifested while merged with you, and it cannot act or be targeted.

entries.name: Temporary Hit Points
entries: You gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to three times your Summoner level. These go away when you un-merge.

entries.name: Enhanced Strength
entries: Whenever you make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} using Strength, you can use your Charisma modifier in place of Strength.

entries.name: Action Points
entries: You reduce the Action Point cost to use any of your Eidolon's actions by 1. In addition, you gain one Action Point at the start of each of your turns.

You and your Eidolon remain merged until it dissipates, or until you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. You can unmerge early by using a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, causing the Eidolon to appear in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you with any remaining Action Points you had.
You can use this feature twice. You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Symbiotic Synergy
entries: Whenever you use your action to cast a Summoner spell while merged with your Eidolon, you can make one Conjured Strike attack as part of that action.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Wrap
entries: When a creature fails its saving throw against your Eidolon's Enwap {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, the Eidolon can cause one of the following effects:

entries.name: Sap Life
entries: The target takes {@dice 2d6} plus your Charisma modifier in Necrotic damage and you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Necrotic damage it takes.

entries.name: Restrict
entries: While the target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Eidolon, it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once) and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Perfect Symbiosis
entries: You can now use your Merge Bodies feature without expending a use. You do not gain the {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} of the feature when you use it in this way.

In addition, while you are merged with your Eidolon, it is able to use its action, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} on its own, acting from your space, causing gooey tendrils to emerge from your body to use its actions. For the purposes of using its Enwrap action, you can each have one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Bond of the Grave Caller
entries: {@i Corrupt your Soul. Call on the Dead}

You have formed an unusual and uncanny bond with the spirit of an undead creature, a taboo choice that other Summoners struggle to understand. Together with your Undead partner, you brave the risks of corrupting your soul to uncover the secrets of life, death and undeath.

subclassFeature.name: Undead Spells
entries: When you reach a Summoner level specified in the Undead Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Undead Spells
rows: {@spell Darkness|XPHB}, {@spell Ray of Enfeeblement|XPHB}, {@spell Summon Undead|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Undead Eidolon
entries: Your Eidolon manifests as either a ghostly apparition or the remains of a corpse. Your Eidolon's stat block changes in the following ways:

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Its creature type changes to Undead.

entries.name: Ability Scores
entries: Its Strength, Dexterity and Constitution scores change to 14 (+2), 13 (+1) and 16 (+3) respectively.

entries.name: Skills
entries: It gains proficiency with the {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} and {@skill Religion|XPHB} skills.

entries.name: Immunities
entries: It gains {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison damage and the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Resistances
entries: It gains {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.

entries.name: Undead Vessel
entries: When you choose this subclass, choose whether it is a Corpse or a Spirit. It gains the following {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} depending on your choice:

entries.name: Undead Fortitude (Corpse)
entries: When the Eidolon drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} as a result of it taking damage of a type other than Radiant, it makes a Constitution save, with DC 10 or half the damage taken, whichever is higher. On a success, it drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

entries.name: Partially Incorporeal (Spirit)
entries: When the Eidolon takes damage of a type other than Radiant, it reduces that instance of damage by half.

The Eidolon can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once). You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Necromancy Expert
entries: The benefits of your Conjuration Expert feature also extend to Necromancy spells. In addition, Necromancy spells on the Wizard spell list are added to the Summoner spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Life Drain
entries: Your Eidolon gains the following {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}:

{@b Life Drain (1 AP).} Constitution {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}: DC equals your spell save DC, on one creature the Eidolon has just dealt damage to. {@i Failure:} The target takes {@damage 1d6} + half your Summoner level (round up) in Necrotic damage. {@i Success:} Half damage.
Either you or your Eidolon gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Necrotic damage taken.

subclassFeature.name: Soul of Death
entries: Your link to your Undead Eidolon has tainted your soul with dark energies, granting you the following benefits

entries.name: Resistances
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison and Necrotic damage, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Undead Resilience
entries: You can use your Eidolon's Undead Fortitude or Partially Incorporeal {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, expending a use of that feature.

subclassFeature.name: Ultimate Manifestation
entries: Your Eidolon gains a benefit based on its Undead Vessel.

entries.name: Corpse
entries: When a creature fails its saving throw against your Life Drain feature, it gains the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the condition on a success. If the creature dies while it still has this condition, it turns into a {@creature Zombie|XMM} under your control, using the following rules

items: {@b Statistics.} It uses the {@creature Zombie|XMM} stat block, but uses your spell attack modifier for its attack rolls, adds your Charisma modifier to its damage rolls and its size is the same as it was before it died.
{@b Combat.} The corpse takes its turn immediately after yours in combat.
{@b Duration.} It dies after 1 hour, or if you use this feature to create another {@creature Zombie|XMM}.

entries.name: Spirit
entries: The Eidolon becomes incorporeal. It can move through creatures, objects and structures, though it takes {@damage 1d10} Force damage if it ends its turn inside solid matter. This damage cannot be reduced in any way.
